parsing_res_list.block_content: Grade: 1Subject: English GrammarTitle: Nouns (Singular/Plural)
Fill in the blanks using suitable words from the bracket.

One day, Bunny, the rabbit was sleeping _____ (preposition) a tree when _____ (article) pebble fell on his head. He woke up. _____ (proper noun of rabbit) thought that a part of the sky was falling. He started running and kept shouting, "The sky is falling! The sky is falling! Run for your lives!"

Many _____(animal / animals) and birds believed Bunny and started running with him. The butterfly who was sitting next to Bunny started laughing. "Hold on, friends!" she said. "Don't listen to Bunny. It was just _____(article) pebble that fell _____(preposition) his head."
All the animals and _____(birds/bird) were amazed by the butterfly's presence of mind.

parsing_res_list.block_content: Grade: 1Subject: English GrammarTitle: Nouns (Gender)
Gender: The gender of a noun tells whether the person or animal is male (masculine or female (feminine)).
Masculine: A noun that talks about a male is called the masculine gender.
Feminine: A noun that talks about a female is called the feminine gender.

Practice Sheet-1 Date: ___ Select the appropriate gender to complete the sentence. Example - The $ \underline{girl} $ wore a skirt to the party. (boy/girl) 1. The _____ is a very tall man. (king/queen) 2. The red _____ is sitting on her eggs. (cock/hen) 3. The _____ is a very beautiful girl.(princess/prince) 4. The _____ loves her cubs. (tigress/tiger) 5. Veena is Mr.Goel's _____. (son/daughter) 6. My _____ has a big moustache. (uncle/aunt) 7. Sheila's_____ is wearing a blue saree. (grandmother/grandfather) 8.A _____ dances in the rain. (peacock/peahen) 9. A _____ is the king of the jungle. (lion/lioness) 10. My sister's daughter is my ___. (nephew/niece) 11. A _____. (fox/vixen) gives birth to a baby fox. 12. My _____ is a handsome man. (father/mother)

parsing_res_list.block_content: Grade: 1Subject: English GrammarTitle: Nouns (Gender)
Frame sentences using the given pair of genders.

Once upon ___ (article) time there lived a king and a ___ (feminine gender-king) in a ___ (common noun). One day, the couple was blessed with a cute little girl and a ___ (masculine gender-girl). The boy grew up and became a smart prince while the girl became an elegant ___ (feminine gender- prince). The prince was very fond of animals. He went to a jungle and saw a ___ (feminine gender-tiger) playing and cuddling her cubs. He felt very happy on seeing this. He returned home and told his ___ (masculine gender-mother) that he wanted to have a pet. Whereas the king's ___ (feminine gender-son) was fond of gardening so she requested her ___ (feminine gender-father) to get her some ___ (sapling)

Grade: 1Subject: English GrammarTitle: Comic Strip
Date: ___ Title: ___ Illustrated by-___ Read the given sentences carefully and illustrate them.
“Look!” I said. “My doughnuts are delicious.”“I got it from the best bakery in town.”I showed it to my sister.
“She wanted to eat all my doughnuts alone”.I said, “No! ” But my mother asked me to share.I agreed and we enjoyed eating the doughnuts together.

parsing_res_list.block_content: Grade: 1Subject: English GrammarTopic: Pronouns
A pronoun is a word which is used in place of a noun.
'This' is used for singular nouns which are close to us.
'That' is used for singular nouns which are far from us.
'These' is used for plural nouns which are close to us.
'Those' is used for plural nouns which are far from us.

markdown.text:
Grade: 1Subject: English GrammarTopic: Pronouns
Date: ___ Reading Worksheet A pronoun is a word which is used in place of a noun. 'This' is used for singular nouns which are close to us. Example-
This is a book. 'That' is used for singular nouns which are far from us. Example- That is a book.
'These' is used for plural nouns which are close to us. Example-
These are books. 'Those' is used for plural nouns which are far from us. Example- Those are books.

All the _____(common noun) enjoyed showing the favourite object.
